item.title: US-Iran strikes: latest developments
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday pledged to stage tit-for-tat bomb attacks on Iranian civil infrastructure every time Tehran fired at ships in the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Trump threatens payback US President Donald Trump threatened to knock out one Iranian bridge or power plant in return for each attack on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, warning he was "not finished" with a war that has already cost Washington $37.5 billion.

item.title: Philippine, Chinese foreign ministers trade protests over South China Sea encounter
item.description: MANILA/BEIJING, July 22 (Reuters) - The foreign ministers of the Philippines and China met in Manila on Wednesday and both made "strong" protests over an encounter between the two countries' vessels this week in contested South China Sea waters.

item.title: Treasury's Bessent welcomes Pakistan's reforms, push to return to capital markets
item.description:
By Andrea Shalal

WASHINGTON, July 22 (Reuters) - U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent met Pakistan's finance minister, Muhammad Aurangzeb, on Tuesday and welcomed the country's progress in implementing economic reforms and laying the groundwork for a return to international capital markets, Treasury said in a statement on Wednesday.

The statement did not address Pakistan's request for a $10 billion exchange stabilization facility, first reported by Reuters.

item.title: How US bid prevailed over China, France, Russia in Saudi nuclear deal
item.description: The Trump administration has made American nuclear technology a major priority with an $80 billion investment in the sector, including nearly $18 billion in loans to Westinghouse Electric Company to develop its AP1000 reactor lines.

item.title: How US bid prevailed over China, France, Russia in Saudi nuclear deal
item.description: The Trump administration has made American nuclear technology a major priority with an $80 billion investment in the sector, including nearly $18 billion in loans to Westinghouse Electric Company to develop its AP1000 reactor lines.

item.title: How US bid prevailed over China, France, Russia in Saudi nuclear deal
item.description: The Trump administration has made American nuclear technology a major priority with an $80 billion investment in the sector, including nearly $18 billion in loans to Westinghouse Electric Company to develop its AP1000 reactor lines.

item.title: How US bid prevailed over China, France, Russia in Saudi nuclear deal
item.description: The Trump administration has made American nuclear technology a major priority with an $80 billion investment in the sector, including nearly $18 billion in loans to Westinghouse Electric Company to develop its AP1000 reactor lines.

item.title: Trump vows tit-for-tat strikes on Iran power plants
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday pledged that the United States would bomb Iranian civil infrastructure every time Tehran fires at ships in the strategic Strait of Hormuz. "From this point forward, any time the Islamic Republic of Iran shoots at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, whether it be by Missile, Rocket, Drone, or any other device or weapon, the United States will bomb and destroy ONE BRIDGE OR POWER PLANT," he wrote on his Truth Social network.

item.title: Lebanon, Israel to hold next talks in Italy on August 4, Lebanese official says
item.description:
BEIRUT, July 22 (Reuters) - Lebanon and Israel are scheduled to hold their next round of U.S.-brokered talks on August 4 in Italy, a Lebanese official told Reuters on Wednesday, as the two countries begin implementing a plan for Israeli withdrawals and Lebanese army deployments in southern Lebanon.

The two longtime foes have held more than three months of face-to-face talks, billed as a way to put a permanent end to hostilities since a new war erupted on March 2between Israel and Lebanese armed group Hezbollah, triggered by the wider regional conflict.

item.title: Iran's Gulf islands: targets in latest fight over Hormuz
item.description: A US missile struck the Iranian island of Larak on Wednesday, the Tasnim news agency reported, in the latest attack to target the Islamic republic's islands in and around the Strait of Hormuz. - Larak - This small island occupies a particularly strategic position at the narrowest point of the Strait of Hormuz, the key shipping route that Iran has choked shut.

item.title: Trump: US will attack Iranian bridge, power plant for every ship targeted in Hormuz
item.description:
WASHINGTON, July 22 (Reuters) - President Donald Trump renewed his threats to attack Iranian infrastructure on Wednesday, saying the U.S. would attack a bridge or power planteach time Tehran targets a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

"Any time the Islamic Republic of Iran shoots at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, whether it be by Missile, Rocket, Drone, or any other device or weapon, the United States will bomb and destroy ONE BRIDGE OR POWER PLANT, including those located next to, or in, the Capital City of Tehran," Trump said in a post on Truth Social.

item.title: Ancient Lebanese city of Tyre added to heritage danger list
item.description: Tyre, an ancient Phoenician city in southern Lebanon hit by Israeli bombardment in recent months, was on Wednesday added to the UNESCO list of world heritage sites in danger. The ancient city, founded some 4,700 years ago, is home to two protected sites listed as UNESCO World Heritage Sites, a Roman triumphal arch and a hippodrome dating from the second century.

item.title: Trump threatens Iran bridges, power as payback for Hormuz attacks
item.description: US President Donald Trump threatened Wednesday to knock out one Iranian bridge or power plant in return for each attack on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, pressing on with a war that has so far cost Washington $37.5 billion. "Any time the Islamic Republic of Iran shoots at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, whether it be by Missile, Rocket, Drone, or any other device or weapon, the United States will bomb and destroy ONE BRIDGE OR POWER PLANT," Trump wrote on social media, reiterating his longstanding threat to go after Iranian infrastructure.

item.title: Trump threatens Iran bridges, power plants over Hormuz attacks
item.description: The warning comes as Secretary of State Marco Rubio says Washington remains open to negotiations with Tehran.

item.title: Iran executes man over January protests, judiciary's news agency says
item.description:
DUBAI, July 22 (Reuters) - Iran executed a man on charges linked to nationwide protests in January, an opposition group and the Iranian judiciary's official Mizan news agency reported on Wednesday.

Mizan identified the man as Mehdi Khaneki and said he was executed for "operational actions" that benefited Israel, the United States and hostile groups, and for what it called crimes during the January protests. It said he also produced and possessed weapons.

item.title: Explainer-Why is a proposed US-Saudi nuclear pact causing alarm in Washington and the Middle East?
item.description:
DUBAI, July 22 (Reuters) - The Trump administration backs a nuclear energy pact with Riyadh allowing Saudi Arabiato enrich uranium and process spent atomic fuel, two sources have said, a move that may worry regional states concerned about weapons proliferation.

Here are some of the main issues at play:

WHAT HAS HAPPENED SO FAR?

Washington and Riyadh have for years been discussing a nuclear cooperation deal that would give Saudi Arabia access to U.S. technology and American companies a chance to win big contracts for Saudi atomic energy facilities.

item.title: Extreme heatwave grips Tunisia as wide power cuts fuel frustration
item.description: In Tunisia long power outages across the country have added to the strain of an intense heatwave, with temperatures as high as 49C, leaving many fearing for their livelihoods. Power and water cuts have affected parts of Tunisia in previous summers, but this year's outages have spread across much of the country.

item.title: US, Saudi Arabia set to sign nuclear agreement: What to know
item.description: The agreement would allow US companies to build uranium enrichment facilities in Saudi Arabia for its civilian nuclear program.

item.title: US, Saudi Arabia set to sign nuclear agreement: What to know
item.description: The agreement would allow US companies to build uranium enrichment facilities in Saudi Arabia for its civilian nuclear program.

item.title: US, Saudi Arabia set to sign nuclear agreement: What to know
item.description: The agreement would allow US companies to build uranium enrichment facilities in Saudi Arabia for its civilian nuclear program.
